text.title: Indexing Strategy
text.summary: Index design keyed to query patterns; partial indexes; composite column order rules; unused index detection.

rule.statement: Create indexes based on actual query patterns. Review `pg_stat_user_indexes.idx_scan` quarterly and drop indexes with zero scans.
rule.rationale: Every index increases write overhead (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE must maintain all indexes). An unused index wastes I/O and storage with no read benefit. The pg_stat_user_indexes view shows exactly how many times each index has been used since the last stats reset.

rule.statement: Order composite index columns by selectivity: put the most selective column (or the column used in equality filters) first. Columns used only in ORDER BY go last.
rule.rationale: A composite index `(status, created_at)` supports `WHERE status = 'pending' ORDER BY created_at` but NOT `WHERE created_at > ? ORDER BY status`. The leading column must match the filter.
